perturbation.original_text: The term “Confidential Information” as used herein also includes the Transaction itself and any information, work papers, analyses, compilations, projections, studies, documents, terms, conditions, correspondence, facts or other materials derived or produced by COMVERGE or its representatives for each other which contain or otherwise reflect confidential or proprietary information provided or developed by COMVERGE in connection with the Transaction, or any other information which H.I.G. knows or reasonably ought to know is confidential or proprietary information of COMVERGE.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Confidential Information” as used herein also includes the Transaction itself and any information, work papers, analyses, compilations, projections, studies, documents, terms, conditions, correspondence, facts or other materials derived or produced by COMVERGE or its representatives for each other which contain or otherwise reflect confidential or proprietary information provided or developed by COMVERGE in connection with the Transaction. It *excludes* any other information which H.I.G. knows or reasonably ought to know is confidential or proprietary information of COMVERGE, *unless explicitly marked as confidential*.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the definition of 'Confidential Information'. Initially, anything H.I.G. reasonably ought to know is confidential, is included. Now, only explicitly marked information is considered confidential. This directly contradicts the broad inclusion earlier in the same definition in Section 1, creating uncertainty about what H.I.G. is obligated to keep secret. The contradiction arises in Section 1 itself, due to the added clause. The term 'Confidential Information' is now ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information Defined . The Parties acknowledge that, in the course of the Transaction, it is anticipated that H.I.G. and its Representatives will receive certain non-public and confidential information, from or about COMVERGE, including, but not limited to financing information, operating budgets, strategic business plan documents, product and services offerings, software platform or any other confidential and proprietary information relating to the Transaction or COMVERGE. All such financial information and term sheets supplied by COMVERGE or its representatives are hereinafter called the “ Confidential Information ”. The term “Confidential Information” as used herein also includes the Transaction itself and any information, work papers, analyses, compilations, projections, studies, documents, terms, conditions, correspondence, facts or other materials derived or produced by COMVERGE or its representatives for each other which contain or otherwise reflect confidential or proprietary information provided or developed by COMVERGE in connection with the Transaction. It *excludes* any other information which H.I.G. knows or reasonably ought to know is confidential or proprietary information of COMVERGE, *unless explicitly marked as confidential*. Any Confidential Information supplied in connection with the Transaction by COMVERGE prior to the execution of this Agreement shall be considered in the same manner and be subject to the same treatment as the Confidential Information made available hereunder after the execution of this Agreement. For the purposes of this Agreement, “ Representatives ” of H.I.G. shall include its employees, officers, directors, financial advisors, staff agents, financing sources, representatives, consultants, advisors or members of any Investment Review or similar committees, but only to the extent such parties receive Confidential Information and excluding, in each case, any portfolio companies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Confidential Information' by excluding information that H.I.G. reasonably ought to know is confidential, unless explicitly marked as such. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the definition within the same section (Section 1) which includes such information, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about what constitutes 'Confidential Information'. The contradictory clauses are located within the Contradicted Text, specifically the sentence 'The term “Confidential Information” as used herein also includes the Transaction itself and any information... H.I.G. knows or reasonably ought to know is confidential or proprietary information of COMVERGE' which is then contradicted by the added clause 'It excludes any other information which H.I.G. knows or reasonably ought to know is confidential or proprietary information of COMVERGE, unless explicitly marked as confidential'.

perturbation.original_text: H.J.G. shall keep the Confidential Information confidential and shall not disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any person other than its Representatives who need to know such Confidential Information in connection with H.I.G.’ S evaluation, negotiation and potential consummation of the Transaction, except with the prior written consent of COMVERGE or as otherwise permitted hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: H.J.G. *may* keep the Confidential Information confidential and *may* disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any person other than its Representatives who need to know such Confidential Information in connection with H.I.G.’ S evaluation, negotiation and potential consummation of the Transaction, except with the prior written consent of COMVERGE or as otherwise permitted hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that H.I.G. 'shall' keep the information confidential, an obligation. The modified text uses 'may', making it discretionary. This directly contradicts the binding obligation established in section 3 and renders the protection of confidential information uncertain. This impacts the entire contract because it changes a fundamental obligation. The location of change is Section 3 and the contradicted text is within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-disclosure Obligation . H.J.G. *may* keep the Confidential Information confidential and *may* disclose such Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any person other than its Representatives who need to know such Confidential Information in connection with H.I.G.’ S evaluation, negotiation and potential consummation of the Transaction, except with the prior written consent of COMVERGE or as otherwise permitted hereunder. The Confidential Information shall be used by H.I.G. solely for the purpose of evaluating the Transaction, and shall not be otherwise used without COMVERGE’s prior written consent. H.I.G. agrees that it may disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of assisting the Parties in connection with the Transaction. Prior to disseminating any of the Confidential Information to any agent and/or representative permitted herein, H.I.G. shall advise its Representative of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information, and shall require such Representative to agree to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and to be bound by terms of confidentiality at least as restrictive as the terms of this Agreement. Additionally, except as required by law, neither Party shall, and each Party shall direct its representatives to not, disclose to any person (i) the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to H.I.G. and its Representatives, (ii) the fact that the Parties are in discussions regarding the Transaction, or (iii) the terms and conditions of such discussions, including the status thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall', creating a binding obligation for H.J.G. to keep the information confidential. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may', making the confidentiality obligation discretionary. This directly contradicts the original binding obligation and the general expectation of confidentiality detailed in the contradicted text, which discusses the need to maintain confidentiality and restrict disclosure to representatives and mandates confidentiality agreements with those representatives. The changed text makes this obligation uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: For the purpose of complying with the obligations set forth herein, H.I.G. shall use a reasonable standard of care, no less than efforts commensurate with those that it employs for the protection of its own confidential and sensitive information.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purpose of complying with the obligations set forth herein, H.I.G. shall use a reasonable standard of care, *but is not required to expend any effort beyond what is convenient.*
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates a 'reasonable standard of care' at least as good as H.I.G. uses for its own information. The added clause allows H.I.G. to do only what is 'convenient,' potentially falling far short of a reasonable standard of care. This weakens the protection of confidential information and conflicts with the standard of care initially stated. This undermines the original intent of setting a specific standard of care for confidential information handling and create an In-Text Contradiction. The location of change is section 5 and the contradicted text is within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standard of Protection . For the purpose of complying with the obligations set forth herein, H.I.G. shall use a reasonable standard of care, no less than efforts commensurate with those that it employs for the protection of its own confidential and sensitive information. For the purpose of complying with the obligations set forth herein, H.I.G. shall use a reasonable standard of care, *but is not required to expend any effort beyond what is convenient.*
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by adding the clause 'but is not required to expend any effort beyond what is convenient.' This directly conflicts with the original requirement to use a 'reasonable standard of care, no less than efforts commensurate with those that it employs for the protection of its own confidential and sensitive information.' The 'convenient' clause potentially allows H.I.G. to fall far short of this reasonable standard, creating a direct conflict between the level of care expected and what is actually required. The contradicted text is the same section as the change was introduced. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition, where a clause placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: No later than ten (10) business days following the receipt of a written request from COMVERGE, H.I.G. shall destroy or deliver to COMVERGE all Confidential Information, together with a certificate executed by the agent and/or representative or principal of H.I.G. confirming that all such materials in H.I.G.’s possession or control have been delivered to COMVERGE or destroyed, except as permitted herein.
perturbation.changed_text: No later than ten (10) business days following the receipt of a written request from COMVERGE, H.I.G. *may*, at its sole discretion, destroy or deliver to COMVERGE all Confidential Information, together with a certificate executed by the agent and/or representative or principal of H.I.G. confirming that all such materials in H.I.G.’s possession or control have been delivered to COMVERGE or destroyed, except as permitted herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states a 'shall' obligation to return or destroy confidential information. Changing 'shall' to 'may' gives H.I.G. the discretion, creating a contradiction. This directly undermines COMVERGE's right to retrieve its confidential information and changes a core obligation into an option. The contradicted text is in Section 7. This create an In-Text Contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership; Return of Information . Except as may otherwise set forth in a written agreement between the parties relating to the Transaction, all Confidential Information (including tangible copies and computerized or electronic versions thereof and also all Confidential Information contained in all deliverables and work papers), including all intellectual property rights pertaining 2 thereto, shall be the property of COMVERGE. No later than ten (10) business days following the receipt of a written request from COMVERGE, H.I.G. *may*, at its sole discretion, destroy or deliver to COMVERGE all Confidential Information, together with a certificate executed by the agent and/or representative or principal of H.I.G. confirming that all such materials in H.I.G.’s possession or control have been delivered to COMVERGE or destroyed, except as permitted herein. Notwithstanding the foregoing, H.I.G. and its Representatives may retain (i) any electronic or written copies of Confidential Information as may be stored on its electronic records storage system as a result of automated backup systems or as may be otherwise required by law, other regulatory requirements, or internal document retention policies, provided that any such Confidential Information remains subject to this Agreement; and (ii) a single confidential copy of all Confidential Information which may be retained by H.I.G.’s internal legal counsel for the sole purpose of prosecuting or defending any matters which may arise from or relate to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that H.I.G. 'shall' destroy or deliver confidential information upon request. The changed text modifies 'shall' to 'may', giving H.I.G. the discretion to decide whether or not to comply with COMVERGE's request. This directly contradicts the original obligation and the contradicted text, where COMVERGE owns the confidential information and has the right to its return, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that, for a period of one year following the date of this agreement (the “Standstill Period”), you will not, directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the majority of the Board of Directors of COMVERGE, (i) acquire, agree to acquire, propose, seek or offer to acquire, or facilitate the acquisition or ownership of, any securities or assets of COMVERGE , any warrant or option to purchase such securities or assets, any security convertible into any such securities, or any other right to acquire such securities, (ii) other than with respect to the Transaction, enter, agree to enter, propose, seek or offer to enter into or facilitate any merger, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring or other extraordinary transaction involving COMVERGE, (iii) make, or in any way participate or engage in, any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of COMVERGE; (iv) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “Exchange Act”)) with respect to any voting securities of COMVERGE, (v) call, request the calling of, or otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management or the policies of COMVERGE, (vi) other than with respect to the Transaction, disclose any intention, plan or arrangement prohibited by, or inconsistent with, the foregoing or (vii) except as permitted in the Agreement, advise, assist or encourage or enter into any discussions, negotiations, agreements or arrangements with any other persons in connection with the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that, for a period of one year following the date of this agreement (the “Standstill Period”), you will not, directly or indirectly, *unless you feel like it*, without the prior written consent of the majority of the Board of Directors of COMVERGE, (i) acquire, agree to acquire, propose, seek or offer to acquire, or facilitate the acquisition or ownership of, any securities or assets of COMVERGE , any warrant or option to purchase such securities or assets, any security convertible into any such securities, or any other right to acquire such securities, (ii) other than with respect to the Transaction, enter, agree to enter, propose, seek or offer to enter into or facilitate any merger, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring or other extraordinary transaction involving COMVERGE, (iii) make, or in any way participate or engage in, any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of COMVERGE; (iv) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “Exchange Act”)) with respect to any voting securities of COMVERGE, (v) call, request the calling of, or otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management or the policies of COMVERGE, (vi) other than with respect to the Transaction, disclose any intention, plan or arrangement prohibited by, or inconsistent with, the foregoing or (vii) except as permitted in the Agreement, advise, assist or encourage or enter into any discussions, negotiations, agreements or arrangements with any other persons in connection with the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The Standstill Period section explicitly restricts H.I.G.'s actions. Adding "unless you feel like it" completely negates all subsequent restrictions, giving H.I.G. complete freedom to act, directly contradicting the entire intent and language of the Standstill Period clause. This creates a clear, unresolvable contradiction. This creates an In-Text Contradiction. The location of change is section 9 and the contradicted text is within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standstill Period . H.I.G. acknowledges that, in its examination of the Confidential Information, you may have access to material non-public information concerning COMVERGE. You agree that, for a period of one year following the date of this agreement (the “Standstill Period”), you will not, directly or indirectly, *unless you feel like it*, without the prior written consent of the majority of the Board of Directors of COMVERGE, (i) acquire, agree to acquire, propose, seek or offer to acquire, or facilitate the acquisition or ownership of, any securities or assets of COMVERGE , any warrant or option to purchase such securities or assets, any security convertible into any such securities, or any other right to acquire such securities, (ii) other than with respect to the Transaction, enter, agree to enter, propose, seek or offer to enter into or facilitate any merger, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring or other extraordinary transaction involving COMVERGE, (iii) make, or in any way participate or engage in, any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of COMVERGE; (iv) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “Exchange Act”)) with respect to any voting securities of COMVERGE, (v) call, request the calling of, or otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management or the policies of COMVERGE, (vi) other than with respect to the Transaction, disclose any intention, plan or arrangement prohibited by, or inconsistent with, the foregoing or (vii) except as permitted in the Agreement, advise, assist or encourage or enter into any discussions, negotiations, agreements or arrangements with any other persons in connection with the foregoing. You further agree that during the Standstill Period you will not directly or indirectly, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors of COMVERGE, take 3 any action that to your knowledge might require COMVERGE to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination, merger or other type of transaction described in this paragraph. The provisions of this paragraph shall be inoperative and of no force or effect if any other person or group (as defined in Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act) shall have acquired or entered into a definitive agreement (approved by the Board of Directors of COMVERGE) to acquire more than 50% of the outstanding voting securities of COMVERGE or assets of COMVERGE or its subsidiaries representing more than 50% of the consolidated earning power of COMVERGE and its subsidiaries. Notwithstanding the foregoing, and as described in Section 14, nothing in this section shall preclude H.I.G. from acquiring securities of COMVERGE in the public markets as a public investor in COMVERGE, so long as such transactions do not violate the applicable United States securities laws. Furthermore, for avoidance of doubt, both Parties agree that the purpose of the Transaction is for H.I.G. to submit a proposal to COMVERGE regarding a possible transaction. Accordingly, until informed in writing by COMVERGE otherwise, H.I.G. may propose potential transactions to COMVERGE without violating any of the restrictions set forth above in this section.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "unless you feel like it" to the standstill period clause. This directly contradicts the intent of the standstill period, which is to restrict certain actions. The added phrase gives the party complete discretion to ignore the restrictions, thus creating a direct and irreconcilable conflict with the rest of the standstill provisions in the contradicted text, as it renders the restrictions unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other and shall be binding on, and inure to the benefit of, the respective successors of the Parties thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement *may* be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other and *shall not* be binding on, and *shall not* inure to the benefit of, the respective successors of the Parties thereto.
perturbation.explanation: The initial clause prevents assignment without consent, protecting each party's interests. The changes allow assignment without consent and remove the benefit to successors, directly contradicting the purpose of the original clause. This fundamentally alters the transferability and enforceability of the contract. This creates a contradiction. The location of the change is Section 22 and contradicted text is within the same section, creating an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous . This Agreement *may* be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other and *shall not* be binding on, and *shall not* inure to the benefit of, the respective successors of the Parties thereto. This Agreement may be signed in one or more counterpart originals, each of which shall constitute an original document. The Parties agree that this Agreement can be executed via facsimile signatures and be binding. This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the Parties and supersedes all prior communications, agreements and understandings between the Parties relating to the Transaction. It is understood that the terms of access by H.I.G. or its Representatives to Confidential Information in connection with the Transaction contained in any data room or website shall be superseded by the understandings and agreements contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 22 modifies the original agreement. Initially, it stated that the agreement could not be assigned without prior written consent and would benefit successors. The revised text allows assignment without consent and denies benefits to successors. The contradicted text also resides in Section 22 and contains the exact clause that has been modified. The change creates a direct contradiction because it alters the conditions of assignment and the benefits conferred to successors, fundamentally altering the agreement's transferability and enforceability. This falls under the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
